seoTitle: Clayton vs Clayton South — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Clayton and Clayton South. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Clayton vs Clayton South: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Clayton and Clayton South.
heading: Clayton vs Clayton South — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Clayton, Victoria and Clayton South, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Clayton South has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Clayton (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Clayton or Clayton South safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Clayton has a flood risk score of 35/100, while Clayton South scores 43/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Clayton scores 53/100 for bushfire risk. Clayton South scores 57/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Clayton or Clayton South?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Clayton South has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.52% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
